questions.text: What is Exposure Bias in the context of Sequence-to-Sequence models trained with Teacher Forcing?
options: The training-inference discrepancy where the model is exposed to ground-truth tokens during training but must rely on its own potentially incorrect predictions during inference, leading to compounding errors.
The decrease in model accuracy when exposing the weights to high learning rates during fine-tuning.
The numerical underflow of the loss function when the model is exposed to long sequences of zero padding.
The failure of convolutional filters to extract features when exposed to low-contrast input images.
questions.correct_answer: The training-inference discrepancy where the model is exposed to ground-truth tokens during training but must rely on its own potentially incorrect predictions during inference, leading to compounding errors.
questions.explanation: During training, Teacher Forcing feeds ground-truth tokens as inputs to the decoder. At inference time, ground truth is unavailable, so the model runs autoregressively, using its own previous predictions as inputs. A single incorrect prediction can steer the hidden state into regions of parameter space that the model never encountered during training, causing cascading errors.

questions.text: How do Luong (multiplicative) and Bahdanau (additive) attention mechanisms differ mathematically and computationally?
options: Luong uses current decoder state $\mathbf{s}_t$ and multiplies via $\mathbf{s}_t^{\top} \mathbf{W}_a \mathbf{h}_i^{\text{enc}}$, which is computationally faster than Bahdanau's additive feedforward layers.
Luong uses previous decoder state $\mathbf{s}_{t-1}$ and adds representations, making it slower than Bahdanau's multiplicative mapping.
Luong attention is restricted to convolutional layers, while Bahdanau is restricted to fully connected layers.
Luong attention uses element-wise subtraction, while Bahdanau uses element-wise division, making Bahdanau faster.
questions.correct_answer: Luong uses current decoder state $\mathbf{s}_t$ and multiplies via $\mathbf{s}_t^{\top} \mathbf{W}_a \mathbf{h}_i^{\text{enc}}$, which is computationally faster than Bahdanau's additive feedforward layers.
questions.explanation: Luong multiplicative attention projects the hidden states and uses a simple dot product, which can be implemented as a single matrix multiplication across the sequence. Bahdanau additive attention uses previous decoder state $\mathbf{s}_{t-1}$ and combines vectors using addition and a tanh activation, requiring more memory and operations.
